one_line_thesis: IonQ is an acquisitive quantum infrastructure company with FY2025 revenue $130 million and Q1 2026 revenue $64.

deep_dive.executive_summary: IonQ is an acquisitive quantum infrastructure company with FY2025 revenue $130 million and Q1 2026 revenue $64.7 million, but deep operating losses and ($283.2 million) of operating cash outflow in FY2025. Cash is $493.5 million after acquisition spending. Moat is unproven, dhando is partial (cash floor near $1.37 per share versus market price), and Marvin proposes watch until filing-grounded evidence shows normalized owner cash improving without relying on warrant accounting or equity issuance. At ~$34.78, Marvin base total synthesis implies about -21.35% per year over 7 years.

decision.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

scenario_contract.rule: Cases must change cited causal operating, capital, probability, timing, or financing drivers—not only a terminal multiple.

change_control.rule: A source fact is locked. Every assumption change requires a reason, author, timestamp, and before/after value.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

method_fit.label: Predictable cash-flow security
applicability_tests: Incremental capital can be tied to identifiable projects, cohorts, or contracted capacity.
Returns on new capital can be separated from returns on the installed base.
Contract duration, volume protection, pricing, and counterparty risk are observable.
Leverage and future owner-funded capital are explicitly deducted.
failure_modes: contract renewal or regulatory reset ignored

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: A complete security value is intentionally withheld while material components are unpriced. Attribution restarts after the first proof-complete baseline.

component_valuation.decision_rule: Operating-only fallback. Add an explicit component_valuation schedule before drawing asset-level conclusions.

valuation_proof.adjustment: Risk and timing are reflected in the low/base/high range.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Owner cash, reinvestment economics, or competitive position remains below the low-case path for two reporting periods.

total_return_panel.error: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.
total_return_panel.return_contract: split-adjusted close excluding dividends; event amounts normalized for later splits; cash reinvested at ex-date close; pre-tax nominal

timeline.label: Q1 2026 revenue $64.7M (+755% YoY) but operating loss ($271.5M); cash $493.5M after acquisitions
Announced pending acquisition expanding quantum networking stack (Skyloom closed per Q1 2026 10-Q)
FY2025 revenue $130.0M (+202% YoY); net loss $512.1M; OCF ($283.2M); cash $1,030.9M
Closed Vector Atomic acquisition — atomic clocks and sensors for quantum stack
Closed Oxford Ionics acquisition — ion-trap chip technology
Closed Capella Space acquisition — SAR satellite imagery and data platform
Closed id Quantique SA acquisition — quantum networking and cryptography
Equity and warrant financings raised ~$3.4B net cash from financing activities in FY2025
NYSE listing via dMY Technology Group III SPAC merger — public float and warrant overhang
Company founded on trapped-ion quantum computing approach (University of Maryland / Duke lineage)

industry.structure: Fragmented early-stage quantum computing market with modality competition (trapped-ion, superconducting, photonic, neutral atom); heavy government and hyperscaler funding; few scaled revenue pools.
industry.share_shift: IonQ gaining reported revenue share via hardware contracts and acquisitive expansion into networking and sensing; IBM and Google remain research leaders with cloud access models.
industry.trend: Consolidation into full-stack quantum platforms; defense and national-security budgets increasing; error correction and networking seen as next bottlenecks after qubit count.

risk_claims.claim: *Primary risk: Revenue growth fails to translate into normalized owner cash before cash is consumed or equity is diluted further.
Technology risk: Trapped-ion scaling and error correction may lag competing modalities (10-K risk factors).
Integration risk: 2025–2026 acquisitions added ~39% of FY2025 revenue but increase amortization and goodwill ($166.3M Skyloom goodwill, 10-Q Note 3).
